summary_en: FY3/26 year-end dividend raised to ¥35/share from ¥20 guidance. Record high since listing. Full-year dividend ¥35/share. Upward revision driven by stronger-than-expected FY3/26 results: revenue ¥55.3B (+10.7% vs guidance), net income ¥3.3B (+22.9%), EPS ¥216.37.
title_en: Notice Regarding Revision of Full-Year Consolidated Earnings Forecast and Dividend Forecast (Dividend Increase) for FY3/26
translation_en_brief:
Pia Corporation announced on 14 May 2026 a revision to its FY3/26 (ended 31 March 2026) dividend forecast. The year-end (期末) dividend per share has been increased by ¥15, from the prior guidance of ¥20/share to ¥35/share—the record high since the company's listing. The full-year dividend is therefore ¥35/share, consisting only of the year-end payment.

The revision reflects stronger-than-expected FY3/26 consolidated results. Revenue came in at ¥55.3 billion, 10.7% above the prior forecast of ¥50 billion, driven by brisk ticket sales in the domestic leisure and entertainment market throughout Q4. Consolidated net income attributable to parent shareholders reached ¥3.3 billion (22.9% above the ¥2.7 billion guidance), and earnings per share rose to ¥216.37 versus the forecast of ¥176.20.

Management stated that the dividend increase was determined by comprehensively evaluating the actual FY3/26 results together with the company's profit-distribution policy. No interim or quarterly dividends were paid during the fiscal year.
